instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Can a Disabled Person get Term Insurance?
Meta description: Term insurance offers many benefits. But is it possible for a disabled person to buy term insurance? Find out more here.
Keywords: online term insurance, term insurance benefits, term insurance tax benefit
A term insurance plan is one of the best investment choices for an individual. It provides them with a life cover for a specific period of time. And in the case of their untimely death, provides their family with some much-needed financial assistance to ensure that their lifestyle doesn’t get impacted. Financial support is not the only advantage that an individual gets to enjoy though. In fact, there are plenty of other term insurance benefits as well.
That said, despite the many term insurance benefits, India continues to be one of the most underinsured countries in the world. And when it comes to disabled people, the situation is even more dire. This is partly because of the misconception that many people have, where they think that disabled people cannot opt for term insurance.
If you’re in the same boat wondering whether disabled people can purchase term insurance plans, then continue reading to find out the truth.
Can a disabled person buy term insurance?
Of course. Contrary to popular opinion, there’s absolutely nothing that prevents disabled people from purchasing either an online term insurance plan or offline term insurance
That said, if you’re a disabled individual looking to buy online term insurance, here are some things that you should know.
When you’re filling up the term insurance application form, make sure to disclose all of your disabilities clearly and in detail. Failure to do so can prevent the term insurance claim process from going on smoothly and can even lead to claim rejection.
Also, you may be required to take medical tests and submit the reports to the insurance company along with reports from a doctor regarding the exact nature of your disabilities.
And finally, if you have taken any tests or made any visits to the doctor recently, you would also have to disclose them in detail as well.
How can disabled persons benefit from term insurance?
As you’ve already seen above, the term insurance benefits are aplenty. And all of these benefits are also applicable to disabled people as well. Here’s a quick look at how a term plan can help disabled policyholders.
It offers financial security to the policyholder’s family
The death benefit payout that the family receives from the insurer upon the policyholder’s death acts as a financial safety net. The payout can be used by the family as a replacement to the lost income stream, for maintaining their current lifestyle, for fulfilling your child’s future dreams, or even towards clearing out all the debts and other liabilities.
There’s absolutely no restriction as to how the death benefit payout can be used by the policyholder’s family. By opting for an online term insurance plan, disabled individuals can adequately secure their family’s future.
It covers medical expenses associated with certain illnesses
One of the many term insurance benefits that you get to enjoy is the fact that you can customize your term plan as you see fit with the help of riders. Riders are essentially addons that you can choose to opt for during the time of purchase of a term plan. They provide additional benefits over and above the base plan.
Take the critical illness rider, for example. The add-on pays out a predetermined sum of money, known as rider sum assured, on first diagnosis of a specified critical illness. This rider sum assured can then be used by you to cover the costs associated with treating the illness. This benefit is provided to you in addition to the death benefit under the base term plan.
It provides tax benefits
By opting for a term plan, you also get to enjoy several term insurance tax benefits as well. Yes, you read that right. According to section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the premiums that you pay towards a term insurance plan can be claimed as a deduction from your total taxable income to the tune of up to Rs. 1.5 lakhs in a financial year. That’s not all. The death benefit payout that your family gets to receive is also completely tax-free in their hands as per section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act as well.
Conclusion
As you can see, a term plan, whether it is an online term insurance policy or offline term insurance, you get to enjoy several benefits. Therefore, if you don’t have a term insurance plan already, go ahead and get one right away. You don’t have to worry about any disabilities getting in the way of securing your family’s future.
https://www.canarahsbclife.com/blog/term-insurance/can-i-buy-a-term-insurance-for-disable-person.html

A term insurance plan is one of the preferred investment choices for an individual. It provides them with a life cover for a specific period of time. And in the case of their untimely death, provides their nominees with some much-needed financial assistance to ensure that their lifestyle doesn’t get impacted. Financial support is not the only advantage that an individual gets though. In fact, there are plenty of other term insurance benefits as well. That said, despite the many term insurance benefits, India continues to be one of the most underinsured countries in the world. And when it comes to specially abled people, the situation is even more dire. This is partly because of the misconception that many people have, where they think that disabled people cannot opt for term insurance. If you’re in the same boat wondering whether specially abled people can purchase term insurance plans, then continue reading to find out the truth. Can a disabled person buy term insurance? Of course. Contrary to popular opinion, there’s absolutely nothing that prevents specially abled people from purchasing either an online term insurance plan or offline term insurance That said, if you’re a specially abled individual looking to buy online term insurance, here are some things that you should know.
1. When you’re filling up the term insurance application form, make sure to disclose all of your disabilities clearly and in detail. Failure to do so can prevent the term insurance claim process from going on smoothly and can even lead to claim rejection. 2. Also, you may be required to take medical tests and submit the reports to the insurance
company along with reports from a doctor regarding the exact nature of your disabilities. 3. And finally, if you have taken any tests or made any visits to the doctor recently, you
would also have to disclose them in detail as well. How can specially abled persons benefit from term insurance? As you’ve already seen above, the term insurance benefits are aplenty.. Here’s a quick look at how a term plan can help specially abled policyholders.
1. It offers financial security to the policyholder’s Nominees
The death benefit payout that the nominees receives from the insurer upon the policyholder’s death acts as a financial safety net. The payout may be used by the nominees as a replacement to the lost income stream, for maintaining their current lifestyle, for fulfilling your child’s future dreams, or even towards clearing out all the debts and other liabilities. There’s absolutely no restriction as to how the death benefit payout can be used by the policyholder’s nominees . By opting for an online term insurance plan, individuals can adequately secure their family’s financial future. 2. It covers medical expenses associated with certain illnesses
One of the many term insurance benefits that you get to enjoy is the fact that you can customize your term plan as you see fit with the help of riders. Riders are essentially addons that you can choose to opt for during the time of purchase of a term plan at nominal extra cost. They provide additional benefits over and above the base plan. Take the critical illness rider, for example. The add-on pays out a predetermined sum of money, known as rider sum assured, on first diagnosis of a specified critical illness. This rider sum assured can then be used by you to cover the costs associated with treating the illness. This benefit is provided to you in addition to the death benefit under the base term plan. 3. It provides tax benefits
By opting for a term plan, you also get to enjoy several term insurance tax benefits as well. Yes, you read that right. According to section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the premiums that you pay towards a term insurance plan can be claimed as a deduction from your total taxable income to the tune of up to Rs. 1.5 lakhs in a financial year. That’s not all. The death benefit payout that your nominees gets to receive is also completely tax-free in their hands as per section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act 1961. The above tax benefits are subject to the provisions stated in income-tax act 1961. Conclusion As you can see, a term plan, whether it is an online term insurance policy or offline term insurance, you get to enjoy several benefits. Therefore, if you don’t have a term insurance plan already, consider getting one . You don’t have to worry about disabilities in all senarios getting in the way of securing your family’s financial future.
